changes.value:
Add @actor mention support to the markdown editing and display pipeline.

## Changes
- `ui/src/components/editable-markdown.tsx` — add `actors?: Entity[]` prop, build actor color/meta maps, wire actor CM6 extensions + remark plugin alongside existing tag extensions
- New `ui/src/components/actor-pill.tsx` — renders `@name` pill with actor color, tooltip with avatar + name + type badge
- `ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` — pass actors from EntityStore to markdown editors

## Design
- Build `actorColorMap` from actor entities (name → color)
- Build `actorMetaMap` (name → { color, actor_type, avatar })
- In CM6 extensions: add `actorDecorations()`, `actorAutocomplete()`, `actorTooltips()` in multiline mode
- Both `#tag` and `@actor` autocomplete coexist — different prefix chars, no conflict
- Autocomplete matches on: actor `name`, raw `id`, and `actor_type` (typing `@hu` shows all humans)
- In display mode: add `remarkActors(actorSlugs)` plugin, render `<ActorPill>` components

## Subtasks
- [ ] Add actors prop to EditableMarkdown
- [ ] Build actor color/meta maps from entities
- [ ] Wire actor CM6 extensions into editor
- [ ] Create ActorPill component
- [ ] Add remarkActors to display mode
- [ ] Pass actors through entity-inspector
- [ ] Run `npm test`
Wire @actor into EditableMarkdown and remark display

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,23 +1,21 @@
-Add @actor mention support to the markdown editing and display pipeline.
+Wire @actor (and all mentionable entity types) into EditableMarkdown and remark display.

-## Changes
-- `ui/src/components/editable-markdown.tsx` — add `actors?: Entity[]` prop, build actor color/meta maps, wire actor CM6 extensions + remark plugin alongside existing tag extensions
-- New `ui/src/components/actor-pill.tsx` — renders `@name` pill with actor color, tooltip with avatar + name + type badge
-- `ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` — pass actors from EntityStore to markdown editors
+## Key insight: markdown fields support ALL mentionable types
+The body/description field doesn't know about specific entity types. Instead:
+1. Schema context provides `getMentionableTypes()` → all entity types with `mention_prefix`
+2. EditableMarkdown registers CM6 extensions for **each** mentionable type
+3. Each prefix gets its own facet, completion source, and decorations — no collision
+4. `#bug @alice` in body → tag + actor decorations, each resolved independently

-## Design
-- Build `actorColorMap` from actor entities (name → color)
-- Build `actorMetaMap` (name → { color, actor_type, avatar })
-- In CM6 extensions: add `actorDecorations()`, `actorAutocomplete()`, `actorTooltips()` in multiline mode
-- Both `#tag` and `@actor` autocomplete coexist — different prefix chars, no conflict
-- Autocomplete matches on: actor `name`, raw `id`, and `actor_type` (typing `@hu` shows all humans)
-- In display mode: add `remarkActors(actorSlugs)` plugin, render `<ActorPill>` components
+## Changes
+- `ui/src/components/editable-markdown.tsx` — replace hardcoded tag-only logic with generic loop over mentionable types from schema context. For each: register decorations (cached map), autocomplete (async search_mentions), tooltips.
+- New `ui/src/components/actor-pill.tsx` — renders `@name` pill (or generic mention pill)
+- `ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` — no longer needs to manually pass tags/actors; EditableMarkdown reads mentionable types from schema context directly

 ## Subtasks
-- [ ] Add actors prop to EditableMarkdown
-- [ ] Build actor color/meta maps from entities
-- [ ] Wire actor CM6 extensions into editor
-- [ ] Create ActorPill component
-- [ ] Add remarkActors to display mode
-- [ ] Pass actors through entity-inspector
+- [ ] EditableMarkdown reads mentionable types from schema context
+- [ ] Loop over mentionable types to register CM6 extensions per prefix
+- [ ] Create generic MentionPill component (replaces hardcoded TagPill for display)
+- [ ] Update remark plugin to handle multiple prefixes
+- [ ] Simplify inspector — remove manual tag/actor prop threading
 - [ ] Run `npm test`
\ No newline at end of file
changes.reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,21 +1,23 @@
-Wire @actor (and all mentionable entity types) into EditableMarkdown and remark display.
+Add @actor mention support to the markdown editing and display pipeline.

-## Key insight: markdown fields support ALL mentionable types
-The body/description field doesn't know about specific entity types. Instead:
-1. Schema context provides `getMentionableTypes()` → all entity types with `mention_prefix`
-2. EditableMarkdown registers CM6 extensions for **each** mentionable type
-3. Each prefix gets its own facet, completion source, and decorations — no collision
-4. `#bug @alice` in body → tag + actor decorations, each resolved independently
+## Changes
+- `ui/src/components/editable-markdown.tsx` — add `actors?: Entity[]` prop, build actor color/meta maps, wire actor CM6 extensions + remark plugin alongside existing tag extensions
+- New `ui/src/components/actor-pill.tsx` — renders `@name` pill with actor color, tooltip with avatar + name + type badge
+- `ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` — pass actors from EntityStore to markdown editors

-## Changes
-- `ui/src/components/editable-markdown.tsx` — replace hardcoded tag-only logic with generic loop over mentionable types from schema context. For each: register decorations (cached map), autocomplete (async search_mentions), tooltips.
-- New `ui/src/components/actor-pill.tsx` — renders `@name` pill (or generic mention pill)
-- `ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx` — no longer needs to manually pass tags/actors; EditableMarkdown reads mentionable types from schema context directly
+## Design
+- Build `actorColorMap` from actor entities (name → color)
+- Build `actorMetaMap` (name → { color, actor_type, avatar })
+- In CM6 extensions: add `actorDecorations()`, `actorAutocomplete()`, `actorTooltips()` in multiline mode
+- Both `#tag` and `@actor` autocomplete coexist — different prefix chars, no conflict
+- Autocomplete matches on: actor `name`, raw `id`, and `actor_type` (typing `@hu` shows all humans)
+- In display mode: add `remarkActors(actorSlugs)` plugin, render `<ActorPill>` components

 ## Subtasks
-- [ ] EditableMarkdown reads mentionable types from schema context
-- [ ] Loop over mentionable types to register CM6 extensions per prefix
-- [ ] Create generic MentionPill component (replaces hardcoded TagPill for display)
-- [ ] Update remark plugin to handle multiple prefixes
-- [ ] Simplify inspector — remove manual tag/actor prop threading
+- [ ] Add actors prop to EditableMarkdown
+- [ ] Build actor color/meta maps from entities
+- [ ] Wire actor CM6 extensions into editor
+- [ ] Create ActorPill component
+- [ ] Add remarkActors to display mode
+- [ ] Pass actors through entity-inspector
 - [ ] Run `npm test`
